channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: President Tinubu reassures troops and Nigerians of commitment to fight terrorism and maintain peace
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu on Friday evening make am clear say him government dey determined to fight terrorism and any wahala wey dey threaten peace and unity for Nigeria. The President yan this one...

item.title: Ng’eno’s Mother Appeals to Ruto to Keep Emurua Dikirr MP Seat in Family
item.description: Johana Ng’eno, the MP for Emurua Dikirr in Narok County, passed away in a tragic helicopter crash in late February 2026. Amid the mourning, his mother has come out strongly appealing to President Will...

item.title: Most Indian cities recorded winter air pollution levels above national standards, CREA reports
item.description: During the winter months from October 2025 to February 2026, a study by the Centre for Research on Energy and Clean Air (CREA) revealed that 204 out of 238 Indian cities had PM2.5 levels above the Ind...

item.title: Akani Simbine to host inaugural Simbine Classic in Pretoria with top international athletes
item.description: Sprint star Akani Simbine has announced that he will host the first-ever Simbine Classic in Pretoria on 28 April. With a World Athletics Continental Tour Silver grading, the event will be the highest-...

item.title: Dodoma to Host Regional Women’s Day Celebrations with Over 6,000 Participants
item.description: Preparations are well underway in Dodoma as Tanzania joins the world in celebrating International Women’s Day this Sunday. The regional celebrations will be held at Mbande Primary School grounds in Ko...

item.title: Cup of Joe Launches First International Stardust Tour in Singapore Featuring Yeng Constantino
item.description: The popular Filipino band Cup of Joe is set to begin their Stardust international tour in Singapore this March 8, 2026, at the Arena @ Expo. This concert marks the start of their first-ever tour outsi...

item.title: IGP Disu Remains Professional Amid Sowore’s Criticism, Says Police Spokesman
item.description: The Police Public Relations Officer, ACP Benjamin Hundeyin, don clear ground say the new Inspector General of Police, Tunji Disu, no go allow anybody emotionally manipulate am or make am take unnecess...

item.title: Three Cousins Born on the Same Day at the Same Hospital
item.description: In a remarkable event at the Royal United Hospitals Bath NHS Foundation Trust on 6 February, three cousins were born within hours of each other. Georgie from Bridgeyate, South Gloucestershire, deliver...

item.title: Tehran Residents Describe Intense Airstrikes Amid Rising Civilian Fears
item.description: Tehran residents endured a night of terror as successive airstrikes hit the Iranian capital, reportedly the heaviest since the conflict began. With a near-total internet blackout in place, first-hand ...

item.title: Kenya Dental Association Pushes for Maternal Health Services to Include Oral Care
item.description: The Kenya Dental Association (KDA) has issued a strong call for oral health to be fully integrated into maternal healthcare services following the sad death of a pregnant woman and her unborn baby due...

item.title: Stephen Hibbert, Known for 'Pulp Fiction' Role, Passes Away at 68
item.description: Stephen Hibbert, the actor recognized for portraying 'The Gimp' in Quentin Tarantino's cult classic 'Pulp Fiction,' passed away at 68 in Denver, Colorado, on March 2, 2026, following a heart attack. H...

item.title: Australia agrees to sell radar system to Canada amid Arctic defence talks
item.description: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has agreed to sell Canada a specialised over-the-horizon radar (OTHR) system, signalling Canberra's technical support for Arctic security, but without deploy...

item.title: Shipping Ministry Advises Ports to Waive Charges for Ships Stranded Due to Middle East Conflict
item.description: With the ongoing conflict in the Middle East affecting maritime traffic, the Indian Shipping Ministry has instructed all 12 major ports to consider requests for reduction, waiver, or remission of port...

item.title: Why Rugs Are Making a Comeback in Home Design
item.description: Rugs are experiencing a resurgence in interior design as homeowners seek both practicality and comfort. Inspired by a scene from the HBO series And Just Like That…, where Carrie Bradshaw embraces rugs...

item.title: Western Cape teacher’s pay docked for unnotified absences
item.description: A teacher at Intsebenziswano Senior Secondary School in the Western Cape has lost a labour dispute after challenging salary deductions for being absent from work for three days without notifying the s...

item.title: Iyabo Obasanjo Declares Independence in Ogun Governorship Bid, Says She No Need Her Father's Backing
item.description: Iyabo Obasanjo, the daughter of former Nigerian President Olusegun Obasanjo, has come out clearly to say that she no need her papa support or backing to run for governor of Ogun State in the coming 20...

item.title: London Mayor Offers Support to AI Firm Anthropic Amid US Government Tensions
item.description: London Mayor Sir Sadiq Khan has extended an invitation to San Francisco-based artificial intelligence company Anthropic to increase its operations in London, following escalating tensions with the US ...

item.title: House Anti-Dynasty Bill Considered Weak by Critics
item.description: The House of Representatives is currently reviewing one of the many anti-political dynasty bills, but critics say the version that passed the House suffrage committee is ineffective. Over two dozen si...

item.title: Baba Ijesha Alleges Setup By Colleague That Landed Him In Prison
item.description: Nollywood actor, Olanrewaju Omiyinka, wey dem sabi as Baba Ijesha, don speak up after him release from Kirikiri prison. Baba Ijesha dey claim say him colleague, Damilola Adekoya, wey people dey call P...

item.title: EAC Summit to Tackle Finance, Leadership, and Integration Challenges
item.description: The East African Community (EAC) is entering a crucial moment as leaders gather in Arusha for the 25th Ordinary Summit of Heads of State, chaired by Kenya’s President William Ruto. The summit comes at...
